summary: n8n-nodes-probe: reverse shell and host reconnaissance to 103.27.109.184:8893 on module load
details: The package masquerades as an n8n community node but the advertised node (ProbeNode.js) is a no-op returning an empty result. All real behavior lives in a top-level IIFE in index.js that runs the moment the module is loaded via require(). On load, index.js executes `hostname` and `hostname -I` and probes internal Docker addresses (172.17.0.1, 172.17.0.2, 172.18.0.2) on multiple ports including 5678, capturing response bodies to build a host/network reconnaissance report, then opens a raw TCP socket to the hardcoded remote 103.27.109.184:8893 and writes the report to it. The same code spawns `/bin/bash -i` and pipes the socket to bash's stdin/stdout, providing an interactive reverse shell that gives the remote party full command execution on the installer's host. There is no configuration, opt-in, or documented purpose for this network traffic.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
